name: Fileless PowerShell Download & Execute (IEX / WebClient)
description: Detects PowerShell Script Block Logging (Event ID 4104) content containing common fileless / Living-off-the-Land download-and-execute indicators such as IEX, Invoke-Expression, DownloadString, WebClient, encoded commands, or AMSI/execution-policy bypass flags. These patterns are frequently used by attackers to download and run malicious code entirely in memory, without writing a file to disk.
query: event.code:"4104" and powershell.file.script_block_text:("*IEX*" or "*Invoke-Expression*" or "*DownloadString*" or "*WebClient*" or "*Invoke-WebRequest*" or "*http://*" or "*https://*" or "*FromBase64String*" or "*EncodedCommand*" or "*Start-Process*" or "*Add-MpPreference*" or "*-w hidden*" or "*-ep bypass*" or "*AMSI*" or "*Reflection.Assembly*")
false_positives: Legitimate administrative scripts that use Invoke-WebRequest or Invoke-Expression for software deployment or automation should be reviewed and, if verified as benign, added to an allow-list or exception.
author: Ghaith
setup: Requires Winlogbeat forwarding Windows Event Log 'Microsoft-Windows-PowerShell/Operational' with PowerShell Script Block Logging (Event ID 4104) enabled via Group Policy or registry, indexed into winlogbeat-*.
note: Correlate alerts with Event ID 4103 (Module Logging) for pipeline execution details, and Event ID 800 for classic PowerShell log confirmation.
